macro.headline: Hawkish Fed, sticky energy inflation
macro.body: New Fed Chair Warsh scrapped forward guidance; the dot plot now points to 1–2 rate hikes in 2026, not cuts. With the Iran-war energy shock keeping inflation sticky, long-duration high-multiple growth de-rates first — and the book is tilted there (NOW, ADBE, NVDA, IOT).

risk.detail: FY guide cut (Jun 4): Americas comps −5% (5th straight), tariffs gutted margins, China stalled. No catalyst until Q2.
FY26 growth cut 10.5→7–8.5%; Watchman/EP/urology soft; mgmt guides flat through Q3. Q2 print is the next risk.
Beat & raised (Jun 11) but sold off; −30% YTD on AI-moat fear (Claude Design), CFO exit, ARR de-emphasis. Narrative, not numbers, drives it.
China export-license tightening + Senate scrutiny. Fundamentals strong (+85% rev) — risk is policy air-pockets, not the business.
No bad news — strong Q1 (+22%), IBM AI deal. But it's your biggest weight + high multiple; leads the drawdown if software de-rates.
Trump MFN pricing deals + import tariffs; Gilead & Novartis signed (90% Epclusa cut via TrumpRx). Pressure weighs on the whole pharma cluster.
FY EPS guide cut ($4.56→$4.07) on deal financing + weak flu season; revenue raised. Defensive — yellow flag, not red.

triggers.name: BlackBerry
triggers.note: Shariah-clean (MX, Jun 19). QNX auto-OS turnaround — but already re-rated to ~13× sales. Only a buy on an auto-cycle scare back to the mid-$5s.

triggers.name: Adobe
triggers.note: Model says STRONG BUY, but the news flags a value trap (no catalyst). Conflict → only add on a deeper flush that widens the margin of safety AND a sign the AI-moat fear is overdone.

triggers.name: Nvidia
triggers.note: Quality at a cyclical peak. Worth adding into a policy/export-headline air-pocket, not at the highs.
